meta.name: Baruch
meta.copyright: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

text.KJV: 1:1 And these are the wordes of the booke, which Baruch the sonne of Nerias, the sonne of Maasias, the sonne of Sedecias, the sonne of Asadias, the son of Chelcias, wrote in Babylon,
text.NABRE: 1:1 I. Letter to Jerusalem Chapter 1 A. Historical Setting Now these are the words of the scroll which Baruch, son of Neriah, son of Mahseiah, son of Zedekiah, son of Hasadiah, son of Hilkiah, wrote in Babylon,
text.NRSV: These are the words of the book that Baruch son of Neriah son of Mahseiah son of Zedekiah son of Hasadiah son of Hilkiah wrote in Babylon,

text.KJV: 1:2 In the fift yere, and in the seuenth day of the moneth, what time as the Caldeans tooke Ierusalem, and burnt it with fire.
text.NABRE: 1:2 in the fifth year, on the seventh day of the month, at the time the Chaldeans took Jerusalem and destroyed it with fire.
text.NRSV: in the fifth year, on the seventh day of the month, at the time when the Chaldeans took Jerusalem and burned it with fire.

text.KJV: 1:3 And Baruch did reade the words of this booke, in the hearing of Iechonias, the sonne of Ioachim king of Iuda, and in the eares of all the people, that came to the booke.
text.NABRE: 1:3 Baruch read the words of this scroll in the hearing of Jeconiah, son of Jehoiakim, king of Judah, and all the people who came to the reading:
text.NRSV: Baruch read the words of this book in the hearing of Jeconiah son of Jehoiakim, king of Judah, and in the hearing of all the people who came to hear the book,

text.KJV: 1:4 And in the hearing of the nobles, and of the kings sonnes, and in the hearing of the Elders, and of all the people from the lowest vnto the highest, euen of all them that dwelt at Babylon, by the riuer Sud.
text.NABRE: 1:4 the nobles, kings’ sons, elders, and all the people, small and great—all who lived in Babylon by the river Sud.
text.NRSV: the nobles and the princes, and in the hearing of the elders, and in the hearing of all the people, small and great, all who lived in Babylon by the river Sud.

text.KJV: 1:5 Whereupon they wept, fasted, and prayed before the Lord.
text.NABRE: 1:5 They wept, fasted, and prayed before the Lord,
text.NRSV: Then they wept, and fasted, and prayed before the Lord;

text.KJV: 1:10 And they said, Behold, we haue sent you money, to buy you burnt offerings, and sinne offerings, and incense, and prepare yee Manna, and offer vpon the Altar of the Lord our God,
text.NABRE: 1:10 B. Confession of Guilt The message was: “We send you funds, with which you are to procure burnt offerings, sin offerings, and frankincense, and to prepare grain offerings; offer these on the altar of the Lord our God,
text.NRSV: And they said: “Here we are sending you money; so buy burnt offerings and sin offerings and incense, and prepare a grain offering, and offer them on the altar of the Lord our God;”

text.KJV: 1:14 And yee shall reade this booke, which we haue sent vnto you, to make confession in the house of the Lord, vpon the feasts and solemne dayes.
text.NABRE: 1:14 On the feast day and during the days of assembly, read aloud in the house of the Lord this scroll that we send you:
text.NRSV: “And you shall read this book that we are sending you, to make your confession in the house of the Lord on the days of the festivals and at appointed seasons.”

text.KJV: 1:15 And yee shall say, To the Lord our God belongeth righteousnesse, but vnto vs the confusion of faces, as it is come to passe this day vnto them of Iuda, & to the inhabitants of Ierusalem,
text.NABRE: 1:15 “To the Lord our God belongs justice; to us, people of Judah and inhabitants of Jerusalem, to be shamefaced, as on this day—
text.NRSV: And you shall say: “The Lord our God is in the right, but there is open shame on us and our ancestors, as at this day.”

text.KJV: 1:17 For wee haue sinned before the Lord,
text.NABRE: 1:17 We have sinned in the Lord ’s sight
text.NRSV: “because we have sinned before the Lord.”

text.KJV: 2:1 Therefore the Lord hath made good his worde, which hee pronounced against vs, and against our Iudges that iudged Israel, and against our kings, and against our princes, and against the men of Israel and Iuda,
text.NABRE: 2:1 Chapter 2 “So the Lord carried out the warning he had uttered against us: against our judges, who governed Israel, against our kings and princes, and against the people of Israel and Judah.
text.NRSV: “So the Lord carried out the threat he spoke against us: against our judges who ruled Israel, and against our kings and our officials and against the people of Israel and Judah.”

text.KJV: 2:11 And now O Lord God of Israel, that hast brought thy people out of the land of Egypt with a mighty hand, and high arme, and with signes & with wonders, & with great power, and hast gotten thy selfe a name, as appeareth this day:
text.NABRE: 2:11 C. Prayer for Deliverance “And now, Lord, God of Israel, who led your people out of the land of Egypt with a strong hand, with signs and wonders and great might, and with an upraised arm, so that you have made for yourself a name to the present day:
text.NRSV: “And now, O Lord God of Israel, who brought your people out of the land of Egypt with a mighty hand and with signs and wonders and with great power and outstretched arm, and made yourself a name that continues to this day,”

text.KJV: 2:12 O Lord our God, we haue sinned, we haue done vngodly, wee haue dealt vnrighteously in all thine ordinances.
text.NABRE: 2:12 we have sinned, we have committed sacrilege, we have violated all your statutes, Lord, our God.
text.NRSV: “we have sinned, we have been ungodly, we have done wrong, O Lord our God, against all your ordinances.”

text.KJV: 2:18 But þ soule that is greatly vexed, which goeth stouping & feeble, and the eyes that faile, and the hungry soule wil giue thee praise & righteousnes O Lord.
text.NABRE: 2:18 The person who is deeply grieved, who walks bowed and feeble, with failing eyes and famished soul, will declare your glory and justice, Lord!
text.NRSV: “but the person who is deeply grieved, who walks bent and feeble, with failing eyes and a hungry soul, will declare your glory and righteousness, O Lord.”

text.KJV: 2:30 For I knew that they would not heare me: because it is a stiffenecked people: but in the land of their captiuities, they shall remember themselues,
text.NABRE: 2:30 For I know they will not listen to me, because they are a stiff-necked people. But in the land of their exile they shall have a change of heart;
text.NRSV: “For I know that they will not obey me, for they are a stiff-necked people. But in the land of their exile they will come to themselves”

text.KJV: 3:1 O Lord almighty, God of Israel, the soule in anguish, the troubled spirit crieth vnto thee.
text.NABRE: 3:1 Chapter 3 “ Lord Almighty, God of Israel, the anguished soul, the dismayed spirit cries out to you.
text.NRSV: “O Lord Almighty, God of Israel, the soul in anguish and the wearied spirit cry out to you.”

text.KJV: 3:9 Heare, Israel, the commandements of life, giue eare to vnderstand wisedome.
text.NABRE: 3:9 II. Praise of Wisdom A. Importance of Wisdom Hear, Israel, the commandments of life: listen, and know prudence!
text.NRSV: Hear the commandments of life, O Israel; give ear, and learn wisdom!

text.KJV: 3:12 Thou hast forsaken the fountaine of wisedome.
text.NABRE: 3:12 You have forsaken the fountain of wisdom!
text.NRSV: You have forsaken the fountain of wisdom.

text.KJV: 3:14 Learne where is wisedome, where is strength, where is vnderstanding, that thou mayest know also where is length of daies, and life, where is the light of the eyes and peace.
text.NABRE: 3:14 Learn where prudence is, where strength, where understanding; That you may know also where are length of days, and life, where light of the eyes, and peace.
text.NRSV: Learn where there is wisdom, where there is strength, where there is understanding, so that you may at the same time discern where there is length of days and life, where there is light for the eyes, and peace.

text.KJV: 3:24 O Israel, how great is the house of God? and how large is the place of his possession?
text.NABRE: 3:24 B. Inaccessibility of Wisdom O Israel, how vast is the dwelling of God, how broad the scope of his dominion:
text.NRSV: O Israel, how great is the house of God, how vast the territory that he possesses!

text.KJV: 3:32 But he that knoweth all things, knoweth her, and hath found her out with his vnderstanding: he that prepared the earth for euermore, hath filled it with fourefooted beasts.
text.NABRE: 3:32 But the one who knows all things knows her; he has probed her by his knowledge— The one who established the earth for all time, and filled it with four-footed animals,
text.NRSV: But the one who knows all things knows her, he found her by his understanding. He who prepared the earth for all time filled it with four-footed creatures;

text.KJV: 3:35 This is our God, and there shall none other be accounted of in comparison of him.
text.NABRE: 3:35 When he calls them, they answer, “Here we are!” shining with joy for their Maker.
text.NRSV: This is our God; no other can be compared to him.

text.KJV: 3:37 Afterward did he shew himselfe vpon earth, and conuersed with men.
text.NABRE: 3:37 C. Wisdom Contained in the Law He has uncovered the whole way of understanding, and has given her to Jacob, his servant, to Israel, his beloved.
text.NRSV: Afterward she appeared on earth and lived with humankind.

text.KJV: 4:1 This is the Booke of the commandements of God: and the Law that endureth for euer: all they that keepe it shall come to life: but such as leaue it, shall die.
text.NABRE: 4:1 Chapter 4 She is the book of the precepts of God, the law that endures forever; All who cling to her will live, but those will die who forsake her.
text.NRSV: She is the book of the commandments of God, the law that endures forever. All who hold her fast will live, and those who forsake her will die.

text.KJV: 4:2 Turne thee, O Iacob, & take heed of it: walke in the presence of the light therof, that thou mayest be illuminated.
text.NABRE: 4:2 Turn, O Jacob, and receive her: walk by her light toward splendor.
text.NRSV: Turn, O Jacob, and take her; walk toward the shining of her light.

text.KJV: 4:4 O Israel, happie are wee: for things that are pleasing to God, are made knowen vnto vs.
text.NABRE: 4:4 Blessed are we, O Israel; for what pleases God is known to us!
text.NRSV: Happy are we, O Israel, for we know what is pleasing to God.

text.KJV: 4:21 Be of good cheare, O my children, cry vnto the Lord: & he shal deliuer you from the power & hand of the enemies.
text.NABRE: 4:21 “Take courage, my children; call upon God; he will deliver you from oppression, from enemy hands.
text.NRSV: “Take courage, my children, cry to God, and he will deliver you from the power and hand of the enemy.”

text.KJV: 4:23 For I sent you out with mourning and weeping: but God will giue you to mee againe, with ioy and gladnesse for euer.
text.NABRE: 4:23 With mourning and lament I sent you away, but God will give you back to me with gladness and joy forever.
text.NRSV: “For I sent you out with sorrow and weeping, but God will give you back to me with joy and gladness forever.”

text.KJV: 4:30 Take a good heart, O Ierusalem: for hee that gaue thee that name, will comfort thee.
text.NABRE: 4:30 D. Baruch Addresses Jerusalem Take courage, Jerusalem! The one who gave you your name will console you.
text.NRSV: Take courage, O Jerusalem, for the one who named you will comfort you.

text.KJV: 5:1 Put off, O Ierusalem, the garment of thy mourning and affliction, and put on the comelinesse of the glory that commeth from God for euer.
text.NABRE: 5:1 Chapter 5 Jerusalem, take off your robe of mourning and misery; put on forever the splendor of glory from God:
text.NRSV: Take off the garment of your sorrow and affliction, O Jerusalem, and put on forever the beauty of the glory from God.

text.KJV: 5:2 Cast about thee a double garment of the righteousnesse which commeth from God, and set a diademe on thine head of the glory of the euerlasting.
text.NABRE: 5:2 Wrapped in the mantle of justice from God, place on your head the diadem of the glory of the Eternal One.
text.NRSV: Put on the robe of the righteousness that comes from God; put on your head the diadem of the glory of the Everlasting;

text.KJV: 5:3 For God wil shew thy brightnesse vnto euery countrey vnder heauen.
text.NABRE: 5:3 For God will show your splendor to all under the heavens;
text.NRSV: for God will show your splendor to everywhere under heaven.

text.KJV: 5:4 For thy name shall bee called of God for euer, The peace of righteousnesse, and the glory of Gods worship.
text.NABRE: 5:4 you will be named by God forever: the peace of justice, the glory of God’s worship.
text.NRSV: For God will give you evermore the name, ‘Righteous Peace, Godly Glory.’

text.KJV: 5:5 Arise, O Ierusalem, and stand on high, and looke about toward the East, and behold thy children gathered from the West vnto the East by the word of the holy One, reioycing in the remembrance of God.
text.NABRE: 5:5 Rise up, Jerusalem! stand upon the heights; look to the east and see your children Gathered from east to west at the word of the Holy One, rejoicing that they are remembered by God.
text.NRSV: Arise, O Jerusalem, stand upon the height; look toward the east, and see your children gathered from west to east at the word of the Holy One, rejoicing that God has remembered them.

text.KJV: 5:9 For God shall leade Israel with ioy, in the light of his glory, with the mercy and righteousnes that commeth from him.
text.NABRE: 5:9 For God is leading Israel in joy by the light of his glory, with the mercy and justice that are his.
text.NRSV: For God will lead Israel with joy, in the light of his glory, with the mercy and righteousness that come from him.
